risk_title: Lengthy and Complicated Coordination with Interim Taliban Authority (ITA)
risk_summary: Both AKF and UNOPS face significant delays in coordinating with ITA line ministries, particularly the Ministry of Rural Rehabilitation and Development (MRRD). These delays affect project approval processes and implementation timelines, with potential for continued obstruction.
evidence_quote: Coordinating with ITA counterparts is lengthy and complicated. Both AKF and UNOPS teams encountered delays when coordinating with ITA line ministries, especially MRRD. It took both teams several months to agree with ITA MRRD on geographical coverage and proposed activities before they could start field investigation and community outreach.

risk_title: Governance Fragility Affecting International NGO Operations
risk_summary: AKF, as an international NGO, faces particular challenges working with the de facto Taliban authority (ITA), requiring ongoing coordination with MRRD and regional offices for activity approval, technical design documentation review, and MOU signing before physical works can commence.
evidence_quote: This will be especially challenging for AKF as an international NGO, as they will need to continue working with MRRD and their regional offices to identify a priority activities list, receive approval on technical design documentation, and eventually sign the MOU before actual physical works could start.

risk_title: Emergency Response Disruption from May 2024 Flash Floods
risk_summary: Flash floods in Northern Afghanistan (May 11, 2024) in provinces of Baghlan, Samangan, and Takhar have created immediate humanitarian needs requiring emergency response measures, potentially diverting project resources and implementation focus.
evidence_quote: To provide immediate emergency response to the May 11th flash floods in Northern Afghanistan, AKF-AFG conducted a need assessment for hygiene kits and chlorine tablets during the week of May 13th. AKF-AFG will continue working with the World Bank team to fast-track procurement and distribution of these short-term response measures while expediting the installation of drinking water and sanitation facilities among the three most affected provinces, namely Baghlan, Samangan, and Takhar.

risk_title: Tight Implementation Timeline Constrained by Winter Accessibility
risk_summary: Both AKF and UNOPS face severe time pressure to complete implementation before winter 2024 when site accessibility becomes limited. This creates risk of implementation slippage and incomplete project delivery, particularly for DACAAR and ActionAid with compressed timelines.
evidence_quote: Tight implementation period before winter 2024. Both AKF (and their implementing partners) and UNOPS will need to closely monitor their implementation plan to avoid further slippage of implementation into winter when site accessibility will be limited. The Implementation period is especially tight for DACAAR and ActionAid.

risk_title: De Facto Authority Registration and Approval Requirements
risk_summary: Project implementation requires registration with the Taliban-controlled Ministry of Economy and approval from MRRD. These de facto authority requirements create governance dependencies and potential implementation bottlenecks.
evidence_quote: as per de facto authorities requirements, the project is now registered with the MOEc (Ministry of Economy, Afghanistan) and an introduction letter has been issued to the MRRD to start the process of signing an MoU with AKF AFG.
